config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menindas orang kecil. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum tiap orang. Masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika hak individu dihormati, lalu kepentingan umum dibangun di atas dasar itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society's interests should automatically outrank individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily "the common good" can be used to excuse abuse, discrimination, or government overreach. At the same time, I do not think individual rights are absolute in every case, since public safety, disease control, and basic law enforcement sometimes require real limits. The key is that any restriction on individual rights should be narrow, necessary, and accountable, not a blank check for the majority. If society's interests always come first, then minority rights become fragile and depend too much on whoever has power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, merugikan kelompok kecil, atau membenarkan aturan yang tidak adil, padahal justru minoritas paling rentan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting untuk hal seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, perlu, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan; bukan jadi alasan untuk menghapus martabat dan kebebasan orang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang menelan hak individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keduanya seimbang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea has too much potential to become a justification for censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the majority decides whose rights are inconvenient. Public health and safety can justify limited restrictions, but only when they are narrow, necessary, and accountable rather than open-ended. If we flip the hierarchy and put society first, individual rights stop being real protections and become privileges that can be taken away whenever it is politically convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan kritik, membatasi kebebasan, atau mengorbankan kelompok kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Memang ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik dan keamanan yang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan memberi cek kosong kepada mayoritas atau pemerintah. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, lalu kepentingan bersama dirumuskan tanpa menginjak martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society's interests should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American point of view, that framing has a bad track record because it can be used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach whenever the majority decides a right is inconvenient. I do think there are limited cases like public health or emergency safety where rights can be restricted, but those limits have to be narrow, necessary, and accountable. If society always comes first, then rights stop being meaningful protections and become permissions that powerful people can revoke.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
